name: Aa'idh ibn Majma
title: Brave Fellow Traveler
shortDescription: A warrior who, along with his father Majma', joined the Imam at Udhayb al-Hijanat and stood manfully against Umar Sa'd's army.
fullStory:
Aa'idh ibn Majma' ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi was a sincere Shia and warrior of Kufa. Along with his father (Majma') and several others, including Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi, he left Kufa to reach Imam Hussain (AS).

Guided by Tirimmah ibn Uday, they joined the Imam's caravan at the stopping place of 'Udhayb al-Hijanat'. Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi tried to prevent them from joining, but Imam Hussain (AS) firmly defended them, saying they were his companions.

On the day of Ashura, Aa'idh fought bravely alongside his fellow travelers. He and his companions launched a collective attack into the heart of the enemy army and were surrounded. After Hazrat Abbas (AS) broke the siege to rescue them, Aa'idh returned to the battlefield with a passion for martyrdom.

Shoulder to shoulder with his father and friends, he drove the enemy back with his artful swordsmanship, but ultimately, due to the sheer number of Kufan soldiers, they were all martyred in one place, eternalizing their names as symbols of friendship in the path of God.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Aa'idh ibn Majma'.
